short: Replacing a Transformer's standard processing layer with many experts and a router picking a few.
definition: Sparse MoE replaces the usual processing layer in a Transformer with a large set of expert sub-networks plus a learned router that directs each token to the right ones. For each token the router selects a small number of experts, often two out of many, so the model's total size grows while the work done per token stays constant. Models like Mixtral and DeepSeek-V3 use this design, with a load-balancing loss to keep tokens from piling onto a few popular experts.
